items.title: AI is exploding code complexity. Here is how Backstage manages it.
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>AI-driven code creation is moving fast, but the management headache for platform teams is real. Here is how the community is using Backstage to handle the complexity and leverage AI safely.

📍 Join us at BackstageCon in Salt Lake City: https://bit.ly/3sp6C1W

📺 Watch the full story and documentary here: https://bit.ly/4du7XIs

#BackstageCon #PlatformEngineering #KubeCon #OpenSource #CloudNative</article>

items.title: Your Kubestronaut jacket is waiting...
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>The blue jacket is the badge of a Kubestronaut. 👩‍🚀

Mastering the ecosystem takes work, and we want to celebrate that in person. If you've reached Kubestronaut status, use your community discount to join us at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India in Mumbai this June!

What’s waiting for you:
✅ Your official gear from Christophe.
✅ An exclusive networking breakfast with CNCF Ambassadors and KCD organizers.

Don’t miss out on the "hallway track" where the real architectural breakthroughs happen.

Register now: https://bit.ly/4mz5UXX

#Kubestronaut #KubeCon #CloudNative #Kubernetes</article>

items.title: Why KubeCon + CloudNativeCon in-person matters
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Collaborating on GitHub is great, but solving a production bottleneck over coffee? That’s where the real magic happens. ☕️

The "hallway track" is where the best cloud native insights are born—from whiteboarding new architectures to hearing raw migration stories you won't find in a PR.

Join the community this summer to build the future of cloud native, face-to-face:

🇮🇳 Mumbai, India | June 18-19
🇯🇵 Yokohama, Japan | July 29-30
Register here: https://www.cncf.io/kubecon-cloudnativecon-events/

#KubeCon #CloudNative #OpenSource #Kubernetes #TechCommunity</article>

items.title: Cloud Native Live Fireside Chat—Powering Private AI: Customer’s View
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>As enterprises transition from AI experimentation to real-world production, Private AI is quickly becoming a strategic imperative. In this CNCF Fireside Chat, Chris Wolf, Global Head of AI and Advanced Services at VMware by Broadcom, and Pankaj Gupta, Senior Director of Modern Application Solutions at VMware by Broadcom, share firsthand perspectives from customers who are successfully building and scaling AI in secure, well-governed, and cost-efficient environments.

From real-world use cases like contact center modernization and computer vision at the edge, to the critical role of cloud-native technologies such as Kubernetes and key CNCF projects, this conversation explores what it באמת takes to operationalize AI at scale.

We’ll also dive into the biggest challenges organizations face -from Day 2 operations and data sovereignty to rising infrastructure and energy costs - and how a cloud native approach is helping solve them.</article>

items.title: Where the Cloud Native community meets 🤝 #CNCF #KubeCon
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Cloud native is a team sport. 🏗️

CNCF doesn’t just host events; we convene the global community to solve real-world problems together. From flagship KubeCons to local Kubernetes Community Days (KCDs), it’s about getting maintainers, end users, and builders in the same room.

Whether you're in Mumbai, Amsterdam, or joining virtually, there’s a space for you to contribute and grow.

Ready to find your place in the ecosystem? 🌍

#CloudNative #OpenSource #Kubernetes #KubeCon #TechCommunity #CNCF</article>

items.title: Cloud Native Live: Falco's Nest & the Evolution of Runtime Security
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Falco, the Cloud Native Runtime Security project, is constantly evolving to meet the demands of modern cloud environments. This livestream dives into the latest advancements and strategic direction of the project, with a focus on two major areas: the new Falco Operator and features that enhance performance and reliability.

The Falco Operator simplifies deployment, configuration, and management across Kubernetes clusters, making it easier to secure runtime environments at scale. The session also covers performance optimizations for high-throughput environments, community contributions, ecosystem integrations, and the upcoming release roadmap.

Plus, don't miss out on an exclusive sneak peek at a brand new project — Prempti!</article>

items.title: The "invisible" work that helps projects scale
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Open source is about more than just code—it needs a solid foundation to grow. 🏗️

Foundations like the Linux Foundation and CNCF handle the "invisible" work that keeps projects sustainable: infrastructure, copyright, events, and certification programs. Without this structure, it’s difficult for large organizations to adopt new technology with confidence.

Strong foundations provide the stability projects need to scale from experimental tools to industry standards.

#CloudNative #OpenSource #CNCF #TechCommunity #Kubernetes</article>

items.title: CNCF: Uniting the cloud native ecosystem
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Building a cloud native stack used to start and end with Kubernetes. But as the ecosystem grew, so did the complexity of managing all the moving parts. 🛠️

The CNCF was built to bring it all together. Here’s how the community is actually helping teams collaborate:
* Governance & Policies: Providing a neutral home for open source projects so they can scale sustainably.
* Mentorship Programs: Programs like LFX and GSoC bridge the gap between maintainers and new contributors.
* Cross-Project Collaboration: Breaking down silos so different teams can build cohesive architectures.

Whether you're a maintainer or just starting your cloud native journey, there’s a seat at the table. 🤝</article>

items.title: How CubeCraft Games scales for millions on Kubernetes
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Running a global Minecraft partner network isn't just about the blocks—it’s about the infrastructure underneath. 🧱

Ziax shares how they use Kubernetes to manage CubeCraft Games, supporting millions of players every week. By moving to a cloud-native stack, they’ve been able to handle massive traffic spikes and maintain a seamless experience for the community.

Want to see more real-world K8s use cases? Check out our full case study library.

#Kubernetes #CloudNative #Gaming #CNCF #CubeCraft #OpenSource #K8s</article>

items.title: Why builders actually go to KubeCon + CloudNativeCon
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>It’s not about the hype—it’s about the "how." Mohammed Naser (VEXXHOST) breaks down why the best part of the event is the deep technical deep-dives with the people building real-world architecture.

Join the community at a KubeCon near you:
https://www.cncf.io/kubecon-cloudnativecon-events/</article>

items.title: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon: Meet maintainers & evolve the tech

Whether you’re here for the hallway track or the deep dives, this is where the real work happens.

Join us at an event near you:
👉 https://www.cncf.io/kubecon-cloudnativecon-events/

#KubeCon #CloudNative #CNCF #Kubernetes #PlatformEngineering</article>

items.title: Connection: Coming together at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>KubeCon is more than just a tech conference—it’s where the community actually comes together to move the needle. 🤝

Ready to be a part of it?

Join us at a future KubeCon near you: https://www.cncf.io/kubecon-cloudnativecon-events/

#CNCF #KubeCon #CloudNative #OpenSource #Kubernetes</article>

items.title: Why KubeCon + CloudNativeCon with CNCF Ambassador Saiyam Pathak
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>It’s about more than just the sessions.
Why do builders keep coming back to KubeCon? Access.

As CNCF Ambassador @saiyampathak explains, this is where you:
✨ Meet the maintainers of your favorite projects.
✨ Swap production stories in the hallway track.
✨ Level up your career with community-led training.

Next stop: Mumbai! 🇮🇳 Don’t miss KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India this June 18-19! Standard pricing has been extended until May 12: https://events.linuxfoundation.org/kubecon-cloudnativecon-india/</article>

items.title: The Path to Kubestronaut: Navigating KubeCon and KCDs
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Growth is a team sport. CNCF convenes the cloud native community to help you reach your next milestone—like Istvan from Accenture, who leveraged both KubeCons and KCDs on his path to becoming a Kubestronaut. 🚀

But not every event is built for the same goal.

🧭 KubeCon + CloudNativeCon provides the breadth and global momentum to see the future of the industry. It’s about the "Big Picture."

📍 KCDs provide the intimacy and local connection to solve real-world problems with the people you can actually grab coffee with next week.

We need both the flagship energy and the local continuity to keep moving forward. Which signal do you need for your next step?

Check out this perspective on why the nuance matters: https://jpaolasini.github.io/thought-process/blog/kubernetes-community-days-vs-kubecon/</article>

items.title: Helios Lesson: Backstage Documentary
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Spotify almost fell into the "internal tool trap" with Helios. Instead of keeping those lessons behind closed doors, they opened them up—and Backstage was born. 🛠️

In this clip, see how moving to a CNCF Open Governance model turned a private experiment into a global standard for developer portals. It’s a masterclass in choosing community sustainability over isolation.

Watch the full Backstage Documentary: https://youtu.be/gJHYTlO0VwA?si=dyXiqjXu4r4vcSBu</article>
